chemo.name: Filgrastim (G-CSF)
notes: Continue through chemotherapy to enhance cytarabine uptake

chemo.name: Fludarabine
notes: Administer BEFORE cytarabine to improve Ara-C phosphorylation

chemo.name: Cytarabine (Ara-C)
notes: High neurotoxicity risk — check cerebellar exam daily
Use central line only — avoid peripheral administration

chemo.name: Idarubicin
notes: Give AFTER Ara-C unless protocol specifies otherwise

nursingNotes: Indication: AML relapse or refractory disease
Daily CBC and infection monitoring are essential
Monitor for cerebellar toxicity: gait, speech, coordination
TLS risk — ensure hydration and uric acid control
Eye prophylaxis mandatory for cytarabine-containing days
High risk for febrile neutropenia — start empiric antibiotics quickly
